raw:
{
  "weights": {
    "R1.1": 0.3,
    "R1.2": 0.3,
    "R1.3": 0.0,
    "R1.4": 0.0,
    "R1.5": 0.3,
    "R1.6": 1.0,
    "R1.7": 0.0,
    "RX.1": 1.0,
    "RX.2": 1.0,
    "RX.3": 1.0,
    "RX.4": 1.0,
    "RX.5": 1.0
  },
  "active_set": [
    "R1.6",
    "RX.1",
    "RX.2",
    "RX.3",
    "RX.4",
    "RX.5"
  ],
  "category_focus": "R1",
  "clarification_needed": true,
  "clarification_question": "Should the improvement focus strictly on sentence- and paragraph-level polish while preserving the current claims and structure (Option A), or also permit higher-level reorganization and reframing of the argument and claims if needed (Option B)?",
  "rationale": {
    "R1.6": "The request to “Improve this draft” most directly maps to sentence- and paragraph-level clarity, grammar, and concision.",
    "RX.1": "Editing can inadvertently shift the level of certainty, so improvements should preserve appropriate hedging and clearly signal uncertainty where intended.",
    "RX.2": "Rewriting must avoid introducing stronger claims than the evidence or the original text supports.",
    "RX.3": "The task should stay within text improvement and avoid expanding into new analysis, experiments, or scholarship.",
    "RX.4": "Language changes should not create misleading guidance or unsafe implications about real-world deployment or guarantees.",
    "RX.5": "Edits should maintain traceability to the original wording so that claim provenance remains clear."
  }
}

parsed.clarification_question: Should the improvement focus strictly on sentence- and paragraph-level polish while preserving the current claims and structure (Option A), or also permit higher-level reorganization and reframing of the argument and claims if needed (Option B)?

rationale.R1.6: The request to “Improve this draft” most directly maps to sentence- and paragraph-level clarity, grammar, and concision.
rationale.RX.1: Editing can inadvertently shift the level of certainty, so improvements should preserve appropriate hedging and clearly signal uncertainty where intended.
rationale.RX.2: Rewriting must avoid introducing stronger claims than the evidence or the original text supports.
rationale.RX.3: The task should stay within text improvement and avoid expanding into new analysis, experiments, or scholarship.
rationale.RX.4: Language changes should not create misleading guidance or unsafe implications about real-world deployment or guarantees.
rationale.RX.5: Edits should maintain traceability to the original wording so that claim provenance remains clear.
